ddls:
CREATE TABLE "functions" (
  "function_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each function */,
  "function_name" TEXT /* Name of the mathematical function (e.g., sin, cos, log) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the function */,
  "function_type" TEXT /* Type of function (e.g., trigonometric, logarithmic) */,
  "implementation" TEXT /* Implementation details (e.g., algorithm, library) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("function_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "architectures" (
  "architecture_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each architecture */,
  "architecture_name" TEXT /* Name of the architecture */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the architecture */,
  "architecture_type" TEXT /* Type of architecture (e.g., CPU, GPU, FPGA) */,
  "clock_speed" REAL /* Clock speed of the architecture in GHz */,
  "memory_size" INTEGER /* Memory size of the architecture in GB */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("architecture_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "precisions" (
  "precision_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each precision level */,
  "precision_name" TEXT /* Name of the precision level */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the precision level */,
  "bits" INTEGER /* Number of bits for the precision level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("precision_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "benchmark_results" (
  "benchmark_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each benchmarking run */,
  "function_id" INTEGER /* ID of the function being benchmarked */,
  "architecture_id" INTEGER /* ID of the architecture being benchmarked */,
  "precision_id" INTEGER /* ID of the precision level being benchmarked */,
  "M" INTEGER /* Input size M */,
  "N" INTEGER /* Input size N */,
  "time_sec" REAL /* Time taken for the benchmarking run in seconds */,
  "perf_GFLOPS" REAL /* Performance in GFLOPS */,
  "result_status" TEXT /* Status of the benchmarking result (e.g., success, failure) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("benchmark_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_benchmark_results_precision_id FOREIGN KEY ("precision_id") REFERENCES precisions ("precision_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_benchmark_results_architecture_id FOREIGN KEY ("architecture_id") REFERENCES architectures ("architecture_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_benchmark_results_function_id FOREIGN KEY ("function_id") REFERENCES functions ("function_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "researchers" (
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each researcher */,
  "researcher_name" TEXT /* Full name of the researcher */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the researcher */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the researcher (e.g., PI, researcher, administrator) */,
  "affiliation" TEXT /* Affiliation of the researcher (e.g., university, organization) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("researcher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projects" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the project */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the project */,
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the researcher leading the project */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the project */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the project */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_projects_researcher_id FOREIGN KEY ("researcher_id") REFERENCES researchers ("researcher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "benchmark_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "benchmark_id" INTEGER /* ID of the benchmarking run */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag (e.g., use case, architecture) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_benchmark_tags_benchmark_id FOREIGN KEY ("benchmark_id") REFERENCES benchmark_results ("benchmark_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "benchmark_id" INTEGER /* ID of the benchmarking result being accessed */,
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the researcher accessing the result */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the result was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the researcher accessing the result */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_benchmark_id FOREIGN KEY ("benchmark_id") REFERENCES benchmark_results ("benchmark_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_researcher_id FOREIGN KEY ("researcher_id") REFERENCES researchers ("researcher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "benchmark_id" INTEGER /* ID of the benchmarking result being commented on */,
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the researcher making the comment */,
  "comment_date" TEXT /* Date when the comment was made */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  "parent_comment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent comment (for threaded discussions) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_benchmark_id FOREIGN KEY ("benchmark_id") REFERENCES benchmark_results ("benchmark_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_researcher_id FOREIGN KEY ("researcher_id") REFERENCES researchers ("researcher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the researcher receiving the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "notification_text" TEXT /* Text of the notification */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Whether the notification has been read by the researcher */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_researcher_id FOREIGN KEY ("researcher_id") REFERENCES researchers ("researcher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "affiliations" (
  "affiliation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each affiliation */,
  "affiliation_name" TEXT /* Name of the affiliation */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the affiliation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("affiliation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "researcher_roles" (
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the researcher */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("researcher_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "researcher_affiliations" (
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the researcher */,
  "affiliation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the affiliation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("researcher_id", "affiliation_id")
)
